title: pauletia affinis
intro: Pauletia affinis is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the caoutchouc tree due to its sap's resemblance to natural rubber. Traditionally, Pauletia affinis has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pauletia affinis are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various skin conditions. Pauletia affinis is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin irritations.
